source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Hansi sticks 12 cubes together to make this figure. He always puts one drop of glue between two cubes. How many drops of glue does he need?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'this figure', so the cube arrangement must be read from the image
image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires identifying cube-to-cube contacts in the depicted 3D arrangement, not just reading a number from text
the task likely involves counting multiple adjacency relations among 12 cubes and mapping each contact to one glue drop
answer_type is numeric with no choices, so the solver must derive the count rather than select from options
multi_solution_policy indicates stable multi-solution potential, but the problem itself still appears moderate rather than highly complex
verifiability: answer_text is a single numeric value ('11')
the target quantity is well-defined: one drop of glue per pair of touching cubes
a verifier can check the result by recounting cube adjacencies from the image

decision_reasons: Core assets are present and usable: question text, image, and numeric answer are all available with no registry issues.
The sample is still recoverable and verifiable enough for collection, so it should not be rejected.
A recorded risk flag indicates possible 3D projection or occlusion ambiguity in the cube arrangement, which may reduce confidence in unambiguous interpretation.
Given the ambiguity risk, this is better queued below cleaner samples rather than promoted as a top-priority keep.
